label: minimal-basis-change
headline_statement: The finite-T rank/support witness \(T=2\), \(K=\{-1,0\}\), \(a=b=1/2\), and \(B^\star=\{C_{10}+C_{11}\}\) refutes Conjecture 2's claim that \(\{C_{10},C_{11}\}\) is minimal in \(\mathfrak B(K)\).
witness_construction_if_refuted: Take T=2, K={-1,0}, a=b=1/2, and the admissible one-column nuisance basis B*= {C10+C11}. With effect-coordinate order (tau_1(01),tau_1(10),tau_1(11),tau_2(01),tau_2(10),tau_2(11)), the retained FWL moment map for (E0,C10+C11) has rows (1/16,0,0,3/32,-1/32,-1/32) and (-1/8,0,0,-1/16,1/16,1/16). The target row (0,0,0,1,0,0) equals 16 times the first row plus 8 times the second, so the one-column recombined nuisance basis regularly identifies the only causal target theta_0. Hence {C10,C11} is not minimal in the proposal's basis class.
